verdict.answer_first: B states the issue immediately: "The bug: this mutates shared state in place"; A buries it after "flagged routing rule" and a heading.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits slightly harder with "Prefer the immutable version unless you have a specific reason to mutate"; B ends more open-ended with "pick based on which model".
verdict.fabrication: A invents context with "The flagged routing rule is for diff/gate reviews"; B has none beyond reasonable examples.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on: it names the bug, shows `next === state`, then gives the replacement function.
